Speed Guide
NameWINGS
Sail #USA 15
ClassJ 112E
DesignerAl Johnstone
BuilderJ/Composites
This page contains the polar curves for all wind speeds. The curves are trimmed to highlight the intersection points between different sails. The legend describes the type of sails, also mentioning the sail area when applicable. Note that the asymmetric on centerline<85%**, as shown here, is a virtual sail representing the blended performance of individual sails of this kind.
**"Asymmetric on centerline<85%" denotes a spinnaker whose ratio of mid-girth (SHW) to foot length (SFL) is less than 85%.
TWS: 6,8,10,12,14,16,20,24
Use the tick boxes below to select the sail type of interest
Headsail, 33.97 m² (id = L/M 23)
Asymmetric on Centerline, 129.73 m² (id = A2)
Asymmetric on Centerline < 85%
© Offshore Racing Congress 2025
Speed Guide
NameWINGS
Sail #USA 15
ClassJ 112E
DesignerAl Johnstone
BuilderJ/Composites
This page contains the polar curves for the specified true wind speed. The curves shown with solid line and the corresponding areas in the legend refer to the largest headsail or asymmetric spinnaker. The curves of the asymmetric on centerline<85%**, shown with dashed lines, refer to the individual sails recorded in the boat's sails inventory. The area and identifier of the respective sails is shown in the legend.
**"Asymmetric on centerline<85%" denotes a spinnaker whose ratio of mid-girth (SHW) to foot length (SFL) is less than 85%.
TWS: 6
Use the tick boxes below to select the sail type of interest
Headsail, 33.97 m² (id = L/M 23)
Asymmetric on Centerline, 129.73 m² (id = A2)
Asymmetric on Centerline 75% , 82.43 m² (id = A0)
